mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward drift in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months signals tightening financial conditions that raise the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive FEDFUNDS trends coincide with positive forward returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above a critical threshold indicating labor market deterioration, forward-looking equity markets price in reduced consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.0% for two consecutive monthly readings
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical instances where UNRATE crosses the 5.0% threshold, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive, or Rank IC < 0.03 conditional on trigger activation over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in INDPRO and RSAFS reflects synchronized supply-side production expansion and demand-side consumption strength, jointly signaling a robust economic expansion phase that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month. Neither series alone reliably predicts the effect; the co-movement is required.
candidate_trigger: both INDPRO and RSAFS show positive 3-month growth simultaneously
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in INDPRO and RSAFS fails to outperform the unconditional baseline 20-day return by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a sustained decline in HOUST housing starts signals an early-warning deceleration in interest-rate-sensitive demand that precedes broader economic slowdown, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter. This mechanism is conditional on being late in the expansion cycle, as early-cycle housing dips do not carry the same signal.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 5% over 3 months
failure_condition: HOUST declines of this magnitude are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of late-cycle instances, or the effect is indistinguishable from housing declines observed in early-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in RSAFS retail sales reflects resilient consumer demand that supports near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week regardless of macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS monthly growth and forward 5-day SPX return remains below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in PAYEMS monthly growth below a critical pace signals weakening labor demand that leads equity markets to reprice growth expectations downward, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 50,000 net jobs
failure_condition: Instances of PAYEMS deceleration below threshold are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or Rank IC conditional on trigger < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising FEDFUNDS and falling UNRATE reflects a Taylor-rule-consistent tightening cycle where policy is actively restraining an overheating labor market, raising the probability of a policy-induced growth slowdown that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter. Neither series alone captures this restrictive policy stance.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising over 3 months while UNRATE falling over same window
failure_condition: Joint tightening-and-labor-tightness instances produce positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or interaction term shows no incremental Rank IC improvement over either single-series model.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, deceleration in INDPRO industrial production signals that higher borrowing costs are already constraining production activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week as markets price in near-term earnings pressure. This effect is muted or absent outside tightening regimes.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising for 3 consecutive months and INDPRO month-over-month growth turns negative
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration during tightening periods fails to precede negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances, or the effect size is statistically indistinguishable from INDPRO deceleration during easing regimes.
